rules.name: comment-why-not-what-edit
rules.evidence: NEVER comment the WHAT of code (the code itself shows what it does)

rules.name: comment-why-not-what-write
rules.evidence: NEVER comment the WHAT of code (the code itself shows what it does)

rules.name: golangci-lint-only-if-go-changed
rules.evidence: golangci-lint run - Only if you modified Go code

rules.name: gofumpt-only-if-go-changed
rules.evidence: gofumpt -w . - Only if you modified Go code

rules.name: proto-format-only-if-proto-changed
rules.evidence: make proto-format and make proto-gen - Only if you modified .proto files

rules.name: proto-gen-only-if-proto-changed
rules.evidence: make proto-format and make proto-gen - Only if you modified .proto files

rules.name: go-test-only-modified-packages
rules.evidence: go test - Only for packages you modified

rules.name: go-acc-full-suite-before-push
rules.evidence: Full test suite with go-acc - Only before final push or if requested

uncompilable: Architecture facts (single-threaded execution, atomic transaction rollback) — descriptive background, not tied to a specific tool call
BeginBlocker/EndBlocker must never panic — critical invariant but no regex-bindable file/function pattern to scope the trigger
Validator determinism requirement (code must be deterministic) — no specific code pattern named in the doc to bind a trigger
dymd CLI command catalogs (querying, tx broadcasting, node/key management, config) — usage documentation listing what you can run, no attached obligation
Security Best Practices list (10 items, e.g. never share private keys, use hardware wallets, verify tx before signing) — end-user CLI operational advice, not Claude Code repo-editing actions
Troubleshooting section (account sequence mismatch, insufficient fees, etc.) — documentation of error resolution steps, no obligation attached
Environment/port configuration and Important Notes (Go version, chain IDs, denom) — reference facts, not actionable
